title: Genetic Programming
text: Home * Learning * Genetic Programming Genetic Programming [1] Genetic Programming (GP), an evolutionary based methodology inspired by biological evolution to optimize computer programs, in particular game playing programs. It is a machine learning technique used to optimize a population of programs, for instance to maximize the winning rate versus a set of opponents, after modifying evaluation weights or search parameter. Contents 1 Evolutionary Programming 2 Supersets 2.1 Genetic Algorithms 2.2 PBIL 2.3 Evolutionary Algorithms 2.4 Evolutionary Computation 2.5 Computational Intelligence 3 See also 4 Publications 4.1 1950 ... 4.2 1960 ... 4.3 1970 ... 4.4 1980 ... 4.5 1990 ... 4.6 1995 ... 4.7 2000 .... 4.8 2005 ... 4.9 2010 ... 4.10 2015 ... 5 Forum Posts 5.1 1996 ... 5.2 2010 ... 5.3 2015 ... 5.4 2020 ... 6 External Links 6.1 Genetic Programming 6.2 Evolutionary Programming 6.3 Genetic Algorithms 6.4 Evolutionary Algorithms 6.5 Evolutionary Computation 6.6 Misc 7 References Evolutionary Programming [ edit ] Evolutionary programming is similar to genetic programming, but the structure of the program to be optimized is fixed, while its numerical parameters are allowed to evolve. The term was coined by Lawrence J. Fogel in 1960. Supersets [ edit ] Genetic Programming is subset of a chain of subsequent fields in Artificial Intelligence . Genetic Algorithms [ edit ] Genetic Programming is a specialization of genetic algorithms (GA) where individuals are computer programs. This heuristic is routinely used to generate useful solutions to optimization and search problems. A genetic algorithm requires: Genetic representation Fitness function performing the Genetic operations of Selection (genetic algorithm) Fitness proportionate selection Reward-based selection Stochastic universal sampling Tournament selection Truncation selection Crossover (genetic algorithm) PBIL [ edit ] Population-based incremental learning (PBIL) is a type of of genetic algorithm where the genotype of an entire population (probability vector) is evolved rather than individual members. The algorithm was proposed by Shumeet Baluja in 1994 [2] . The algorithm is simpler than a standard genetic algorithm, and in many cases leads to better results than a standard genetic algorithm [3] . Evolutionary Algorithms [ edit ] Genetic algorithms belong to the larger class of evolutionary algorithms (EA). An EA uses some mechanisms inspired by biological evolution : reproduction , mutation , recombination , and selection . EAs are individual components that participate in an artificial evolution (AE). Evolutionary Computation [ edit ] An evolutionary algorithm (EA) is subset of evolutionary computation , a generic population-based metaheuristic optimization algorithm. Evolutionary computation, introduced by John Henry Holland in the 1970s and more popular since 1990s mimics the population-based sexual evolution through reproduction of generations. Computational Intelligence [ edit ] Computational Intelligence (CI) is a set of Nature-inspired computational methodologies and approaches and field of Artificial Intelligence . It primarily includes many-valued logic or Fuzzy logic , Neural Networks , Evolutionary Computation, swarm intelligence and Artificial immune system .
